title: Schoenoplectus hotarui
taxonomy: Schoenoplectus hotarui is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It is a type of sedge, which is a common term used to describe plants in the genus Schoenoplectus. The plant is classified as a monocot, meaning it has one cotyledon or seed leaf, and it is a member of the monocot order Poales. Schoenoplectus hotarui is often found in wetland environments, where it grows in dense stands. The classification of Schoenoplectus hotarui is further refined through its binomial nomenclature, with the species name hotarui indicating its unique characteristics.

intro: Schoenoplectus hotarui, also known as Hotarui sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the cattail family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Japan, to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Schoenoplectus hotarui include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Japanese herbal medicine, Schoenoplectus hotarui is often used to make preparations such as teas, infusions, and dried powders, which are used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
